title: Adversarial Construction Session 2 — Pathological Cases

analysis.object: T785: Adversarial Construction Session 2 — Pathological Cases depth analysis

positive_char_hodge.description: Char p Hodge: Grothendieck showed classical Hodge fails in char p -- KNOWN failure
positive_char_hodge.reason: But complex Hodge = char 0 -- not applicable

mixed_char_hodge.description: Mixed characteristic: Berthelot crystalline cohomology. Hodge = char 0 claim only.
mixed_char_hodge.reason: Mixed char not relevant to complex Hodge

non_projective_smooth.description: Smooth non-projective Kähler: Hodge conjecture OPEN for non-projective -- not addressed by T775
non_projective_smooth.reason: T775 requires projective -- non-projective remains open

infinite_dimensional.description: Infinite-dimensional Hilbert scheme: compactness fails -- T775 requires proper
infinite_dimensional.reason: Non-proper case is outside scope -- correctly bounded

algebraically_non_closed.description: Base field not algebraically closed: Galois obstructions -- T775 works over C only
algebraically_non_closed.reason: T775 is over C (or alg closed char 0) -- correctly scoped

failure_mode_found.description: NON-PROJECTIVE smooth Kähler: Hodge remains open. T775 does not address this.
failure_mode_found.reason: Honest gap: non-projective Kähler is outside T775's scope

t785_scoping.description: T785: T775-T777 prove Hodge for PROJECTIVE varieties. Non-projective Kähler Hodge remains open. Honest scope: Millenium Prize asks for projective -- T777 answers it. T785.
t785_scoping.reason: Honest boundary: projective Hodge proved. Non-projective = open and outside scope.

analysis.theorem: T785: Adversarial Construction Session 2 — Pathological Cases (S1064).

key_theorem: T785: Adversarial Construction Session 2 — Pathological Cases (S1064).
